LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO MAY-23-2025 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, ALTERNATIVELY, FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT. PLAINTIFF FILED A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AND DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION WAS DIRECTED TO THE SUPERSEDED ORIGINAL COMPLAINT. (SEE STATE COMPENSATION INS. FUND V. SUPERIOR COURT (2010) 184 CAL.APP.4 TH 1124, 1131 ["BECAUSE THERE IS BUT ONE COMPLAINT IN A CIVIL ACTION [CITATION], THE FILING OF AN AMENDED COMPLAINT MOOTS A MOTION DIRECTED TO A PRIOR COMPLAINT."].) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/HEK)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-31-2025 Off Calendar

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Off calendar. Dismissal of entire action filed on July 17, 2025.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-06-2025 Continued To Mar-02-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar May-23-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, For Summary Adjudication.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Off calendar as moot. Plaintiff filed a first amended complaint and defendant's motion for summary judgment or alternatively, summary adjudication was directed to the superseded original complaint. (See State Compensation Ins. Fund v. Superior Court (2010) 184 Cal.App.4 th 1124, 1131 ["Because there is but one complaint in a civil action [citation], the filing of an amended complaint moots a motion directed to a prior complaint."].)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/HEK)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File A First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-23-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF HECTOR BARRAGAN's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File A First Amended Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Off calendar as moot. Plaintiff filed the first amended complaint on May 7, 2025.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar May-02-2025 Continued To May-23-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, For Summary Adjudication.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Siyun Yang, Esq., of Quill and Arrow LLP, (310) 933-4271, for Plaintiff.
Cameron Major, Esq., of Erskine Law, APC, (949) 777-6032, for Defendant.

The matter is discussed. At conclusion of discussion, the court's tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant General Motors LLC's motion for summary judgment is continued on the court's own motion to May 23, 2025. The court finds good cause to continue this matter to a date within 30 days of trial. (See CCP 437c(a)(3).) The continuance will postpone hearing until plaintiff Hector Chavez Barragan has an opportunity to seek a continuance of the trial date in this matter; obtaining a continuance is a condition of the court's companion order granting leave to amend and whether the complaint is amended will determine whether the summary judgment motion is moot.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/CVA)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File A First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-02-2025 Continued To May-23-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES: RE:PLAINTIFF HECTOR BARRAGAN'S, AN INDIVIDUAL MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File A First Amended Complaint.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Siyun Yang, Esq., of Quill and Arrow LLP, (310) 933-4271, for Plaintiff.
Cameron Major, Esq., of Erskine Law, APC, (949) 777-6032, for Defendant.

The matter is discussed. At the conclusion of discussion, the court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Hector Chavez Barragan's motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is granted, conditioned on Barragan's first obtaining at least a six-month continuance of the trial date in this matter with accompanying discovery deadline extensions.

***SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING***

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/CVA)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File A First Amended Complaint

Continued on the court's own motion to May 2, 2025 to join the summary judgment motion on calendar that day.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. =(301/CVA)
